prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a nearly black frame with only a small bottom-right “90’s commercials” style watermark visible.

It then cuts to a product display: several Pine-Sol bottles arranged in a receding line from left to right. The closest bottle is large in the foreground with a yellow cap and a green/yellow “Pine-Sol” label. Behind it are smaller bottles, some with large red “2” graphics on the front or positioned in front of the bottles. The background is dark and studio-like, with reflective lighting on the bottles.

Next, the setting changes to an interior home space, likely a kitchen area. A large white range hood dominates the frame, mounted against a blue wall. Small green pine-tree decorations appear on or near the hood. A shelf with jars or containers is visible below, and wooden ceiling beams are visible above.

The commercial then cuts to a bathroom floor seen from above. The floor is white tile with small dark diamond accents. Around the edges are bathroom fixtures: part of a bathtub, a pedestal sink base, and a toilet. Large yellow block letters spelling “STAPH” are overlaid or drawn across the tile floor, visually suggesting a germ or contamination concept.

A close-up of a Pine-Sol bottle follows. The label fills most of the frame, with “Pine-Sol” in large yellow letters on a green background. Along the bottom of the label, smaller text includes “CLEANS • DISINFECTS • DEODOR…” though the final word is partly cut off. To the right of the bottle, a three-dimensional green word “SOLVED” appears, as if extending from or beside the product name.

The next shot shows a circular, cloudy yellow-brown stain or dirty patch on a light tiled surface. The image is soft and somewhat abstract, so the exact substance is unclear, but it appears to represent dirt, residue, or contamination on a floor.

The final frame shows large three-dimensional text reading “Pine-Solved” on a pale tiled floor. “Pine” and “-Sol” are in bold yellow letters, while “ved” is green, forming the phrase visually. A small green object or blur appears near the bottom edge, but it is not clear what it is. The bottom-right watermark remains visible.
